integration.note: run SKILL is a prompt; the deterministic seam is the brief_intake helper (unit + CLI tested)

behaviors.behavior: AC1: full brief with persistence=yes -> profile supabase-nextjs, deploy jelastic-dev, and none of the four wizard questions re-asked
AC1: full brief with persistence=no -> vite-hono (zero-signup default), deploy none, no supabase env
AC1: supabase env vars asked ONLY on web + persistence (local DB or web-no-DB -> no env questions)
AC2: partial brief suppresses the answered wizard questions and asks ONLY the gap; missing persistence leaves profile null
AC3 (regression): no brief -> has_brief False, all four questions remain, legacy interview unchanged
behaviors.evidence: test_no_brief_all_questions_remain PASSED + full run-plugin suite 362 green (no-brief path)

behaviors.behavior: Conservative normalization: synonyms map to canonical tokens; ambiguous plain-language answers fall through to remaining (never guessed)
io-boundary round-trip: write brief JSON -> file -> intake -> assert profile/deploy/no-re-ask
io-boundary human/Windows-edited file: BOM + CRLF brief still parses to the right profile
Security: a non-brief-suffix path is never slurped (@/etc/passwd-style path cannot leak file contents into the prompt)
Robustness: malformed / empty / wrong-shape (JSON array or scalar) brief degrades to the legacy interview, never crashes (found by io-boundary probe, then fixed)
Load shapes: dict, inline JSON, inline key:value payload, JSON file, @-prefixed path, plain text
CLI: --brief prints the intake JSON (profile + has_brief True); no --brief prints has_brief False with all questions remaining

Review MUST-FIX: an explicit but unreadable file reference (missing/oversized/OSError/disallowed @-path or bare brief-suffix path) returns None -> legacy interview, never description=<the path string>
Review SHOULD-FIX: description is always str|None (a non-string description number/list is coerced to str, never a silent non-string counted as answered)
Review SHOULD-FIX: Supabase env labels are the NEXT_PUBLIC_-prefixed build vars from the profile (not the bare SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY that mis-map build)
Review SHOULD-FIX: mixed inline text keeps its free-text prose as description (not dropped when a key:value line matches); nested fenced JSON parses in full
